text: Non-heme iron enzymes utilizing dioxygen to produce high-spin iron(IV)-oxo species that catalyze numerous important and challenging oxidation reactions are among the most biologically and medically significant catalysts. Synthetic chemists have for decades attempted to mimic these enzymes and have thus far had limited success in mimicking enzyme-like activation and subsequent production of high-spin iron(IV)=O species. Here we present a MOF material bearing iron(II) centers that mimic the first coordination sphere of iron found in α-ketoglutarate-dependent dioxygenases. These materials allow for activation of O2 to high-spin iron(IV)=O at low temperature as monitored by in situ DRIFTS, variable-field Moessbauer spectroscopy, Fe Kβ XES and NRVS. In addition to being applicable catalysts in a heterogeneously catalyzed system of cyclohexane oxidation, the iron centers in this material can catalyze the homogeneous stoichiometric oxidation of ethane with O2 to produce ethanol.
